source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the frame assembly of a 2025 technical-college student-robotics competition robot, delivered as a multi-part assembly because a robot frame of this class is by construction a built-up extrusion + sheet structure composed of a base frame, vertical uprights, a payload deck, a top cross-brace, and wheel mounts. The self-weight + payload shock, top horizontal impact, payload-deck deflection, and tilt stability pass/fail all reference the assembled frame geometry.

The robot must fit within an approximate 1 m^3 starting envelope (1000 mm x 1000 mm x 1000 mm). The per-team combined mass of all robots must be in the range 20-25 kg; this design targets a single robot of mass 22 kg. Pneumatic actuators, if used, must not exceed a gauge pressure of 0.5 MPa (500 kPa). Field obstacles include stair-like steps and ramps defined by the 2025 rulebook field CAD; the robot must be able to traverse at least a 50 mm step and a 15-degree incline.

Assembly layout. (A) Base frame: rectangular extrusion base serving as primary lower load path. (B) Upright: vertical extrusion columns rising from base to top (>= 4 for rectangular layout). (C) Payload deck: horizontal sheet/extrusion deck supporting the 30 kg payload case; its mid-span deflection under 2.5 g is a pass/fail metric. (D) Top cross-brace: transverse brace at the top reacting the 150 N horizontal impact. (E) Wheel mount: bolted/bonded plate carrying wheel axles at the base (>= 3 for 3/4-wheel layouts).

The frame must carry self-weight plus a 30 kg payload-carrying case (total 52 kg) under 2.5 g vertical equivalent shock with no permanent yield. The frame must also survive a 150 N horizontal impact applied at the top of the robot without visible deformation. Under the 2.5 g self-weight + payload case, the maximum deflection of the payload deck must not exceed 5 mm. The robot must satisfy a static tilt test of 30 degrees in any direction without tipping.

Assembly policy. Mass cap (R1) is a whole-frame sum. Payload-deck deflection (R2) is a function of deck sheet + upright restraint. Yield under self-weight + payload and under top horizontal impact (R3) depends on base + upright + cross-brace + deck coupling. Tilt-to-tip (R4) is a whole-robot kinematic property from assembled CG and wheel track. No part evaluated alone has meaningful pass/fail.

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary: linear static structural FEA for the two main load cases. The pneumatic pressure cap is a component-cert check. Traversal over the 50 mm step and 15-degree incline is a kinematic check combining wheel size, ground clearance, and CG, performed with CAD geometry (non-FEA). Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required.

Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the robot frame, a mass budget, and a report listing robot mass in kg, max von Mises stress per case in MPa, max payload deck deflection in mm, and tilt-to-tip angle in deg.
short_prompt:
Design the frame assembly of a 2025 technical-college student-robotics competition robot, delivered as a built-up extrusion + sheet multi-part assembly: base frame + >= 4 uprights + payload deck + top cross-brace + >= 3 wheel mounts. Starting envelope ~1 m^3 (1000 x 1000 x 1000 mm). Per-team combined robot mass 20-25 kg (this design targets 22 kg). Pneumatics <= 0.5 MPa (500 kPa). Must traverse a 50 mm step and a 15-degree incline per 2025 rulebook field CAD.

Load cases.
- LC1 self-weight + payload: 22 kg frame + 30 kg payload case = 52 kg under 2.5 g vertical, no permanent yield.
- LC2 top horizontal impact: 150 N at the top of the robot, no visible deformation.
- LC3 payload-deck deflection under LC1 <= 5 mm.
- LC4 static tilt test: 30 degrees in any direction without tipping.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Step and ramp traversal are CAD geometry checks, not FEA.

prompt.title: Technical-college student robot frame as multi-part assembly
prompt.brief: 22 kg robot frame fitting 1 m^3: base frame + uprights + payload deck + top cross-brace + wheel mounts; pneumatic <= 0.5 MPa, 50 mm step + 15 deg incline traversal, 30 deg tilt stable.

parts.name: base_frame
parts.role: rectangular extrusion base forming lower load path

parts.name: payload_deck
parts.role: horizontal deck supporting the 30 kg payload case; deflection is pass/fail

parts.name: top_cross_brace
parts.role: transverse brace at top reacting horizontal impact

parts.name: wheel_mount
parts.role: bolted plate carrying wheel axles at base

load_cases.name: top_horizontal_impact
load_cases.description: horizontal force at top of robot

output_format.body_count: multi-body (base + uprights + payload deck + top brace + wheel mounts)

interfaces.load_path: vertical load path from base into uprights
payload inertia reacted from deck into uprights - conditions R2 deck deflection
top horizontal impact reacted from cross-brace into uprights into base
wheel contact reactions at base; conditions tilt geometry

fem_check_policy.rationale: Mass cap, payload-deck deflection, self-weight + payload yield, top-impact yield, and tilt-to-tip all couple base + upright + deck + cross-brace + wheel-mount families. No part evaluated alone has meaningful pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part_is_inherent: A student-robotics robot frame is intrinsically a built-up extrusion + sheet assembly; payload-deck stiffness, top-impact yield, and tilt all reference assembled geometry.

eval_coverage: payload deck stiffness at B-C interface
horizontal top impact via top-brace to base path
step/ramp traversal kinematics from assembled CG + wheels
